data.title: UPDATE: Add has-tag filter + brainstorm note tests to sprint-epic-selection-display test
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Implementation
Add 2 new structural test functions to tests/skills/test-sprint-epic-selection-display.sh:

1. test_sprint_uses_has_tag_filter:
   - Assert Phase 1 "If No Primary Ticket ID Provided" block contains '--has-tag=brainstorm:complete'
   - Assert '--min-children=1' does NOT appear in the Phase 1 filter invocation context
   - Given: sprint SKILL.md Phase 1 block; When: grep for filter flag; Then: has-tag present, min-children absent
   - This test FAILS before Task 2 swaps the filter (RED)

2. test_sprint_hidden_epics_note_references_brainstorm:
   - Assert hidden-epics note text contains '/dso:brainstorm'
   - Assert hidden-epics note does NOT reference '0 children' as the filter rationale
   - Given: sprint SKILL.md hidden-epics note; When: grep for string; Then: /dso:brainstorm present, old wording absent
   - This test FAILS before Task 2 updates the note (RED)

Both tests follow the established structural-boundary pattern in this file (grep for presence of structural tokens, not prose wording).

Update .test-index: add [test_sprint_uses_has_tag_filter] RED marker to the existing plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md entry.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes after both tasks complete
- [ ] test_sprint_uses_has_tag_filter fails before Task 2 (RED phase confirmed)
  Verify: grep for --has-tag=brainstorm:complete fails before SKILL.md update
- [ ] .test-index updated with RED marker [test_sprint_uses_has_tag_filter] for sprint/SKILL.md
  Verify: grep '\[test_sprint_uses_has_tag_filter\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
- [ ] test_sprint_hidden_epics_note_references_brainstorm fails before Task 2 (RED)
  Verify: grep for /dso:brainstorm in hidden-epics note fails before SKILL.md update
- [ ] Both test functions run as part of test-sprint-epic-selection-display.sh suite
  Verify: bash tests/skills/test-sprint-epic-selection-display.sh includes new function names
